paragraphs.en: Elephants never forget' — or so they say — and that piece of folklore seems to have some foundation. The African savanna elephant is distributed across 37 African countries. They move between a variety of habitats, including forests, grasslands, woodlands, wetlands and agricultural land. They sometimes travel more than sixty miles to find food or water, and are very good at working out where other elephants are — even when they are out of sight.
paragraphs.zh: “大象永不忘记”——人们都这么说——而这段民间传说似乎有一定根据。非洲草原象分布于37个非洲国家。它们在森林、草原、林地、湿地和农田等多种栖息地之间迁徙。它们有时会跋涉六十多英里去寻找食物或水，并且非常擅长判断其他大象的位置——即使在看不见它们的时候。

paragraphs.en: Using tracking devices, researchers have shown that they have 'remarkable spatial acuity.' When finding their way to waterholes, they headed off in exactly the right direction, on one occasion from a distance of roughly thirty miles. What is more, they almost always seem to choose the nearest waterhole. The researchers are convinced that the elephants always know precisely where they are in relation to all the resources they need.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员使用跟踪设备证明，它们具有“非凡的空间敏锐度”。在寻找通往水坑的路时，它们会朝着完全正确的方向前进，有一次从大约三十英里外就开始了。更重要的是，它们似乎几乎总是选择最近的水坑。研究人员确信，大象始终精确地知道自己相对于所需一切资源的位置。

paragraphs.en: Although the cues used by African elephants for long-distance navigation are not yet understood, smell may well play a part. Elephants are very choosy eaters, but until recently little was known about how they selected their food. One possibility was that they merely used their eyes and tried out the plants they found, but that would probably result in a lot of wasted time and energy, not least because their eyesight is actually not very good.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管非洲象用于长途导航的线索尚不为人知，但嗅觉很可能发挥了作用。大象是非常挑食的动物，但直到最近，人们对它们如何选择食物还知之甚少。一种可能是它们仅仅用眼睛看，然后尝试找到的植物，但这很可能会浪费大量时间和精力，尤其是因为它们的视力其实并不好。

paragraphs.en: The volatile chemicals produced by plants can be carried a long way, and they are very characteristic: Each plant or tree has its own particular odor signature. New research suggests that smell is a crucial factor in guiding elephants to the best food resources. The researchers first established what kinds of plant the elephants preferred, then set up a 'food station' experiment based only on smell. Free-ranging elephants presumably use this information to locate their preferred food.
paragraphs.zh: 植物产生的挥发性化学物质可以传播很远，而且极具特征：每种植物或树木都有自己独特的气味印记。新的研究表明，气味是引导大象找到最佳食物资源的关键因素。研究人员首先确定了大象偏好哪些种类的植物，然后仅基于气味设置了一个“食物站”实验。自由放养的大象大概就是利用这些信息来定位它们偏爱的食物。
